query: hey we're finally moving off RxJava in our android app — the UserRepository is the worst offender, it's got like 12 methods all returning Single or Observable and 8 viewmodels depend on it. where do i even start
our build.gradle still pulls in retrofit 2.6 and we want to switch the whole networking layer to ktor. the :network module has about 15 service files. should we do it all at once or piece by piece
i need to convert LoginFragment.xml to compose. it uses data binding right now and the layout is pretty complex — nested ConstraintLayouts with a bunch of custom attributes
we have a DateUtils.java that uses java.util.Date everywhere and i want to replace it with kotlinx-datetime. it's a pure utility class, no DI, called from maybe 20 places across the app
trying to move our :domain module to KMP so ios can share it. the module uses hilt for DI and retrofit for network calls — is that even possible without a full rewrite
our old java code uses AsyncTask in a few adapters and i want to modernize it — not sure if coroutines is the right call or if we should just use executors
we're upgrading from view binding to data binding in the settings screen — or wait, actually the other way, from data binding to view binding. there's like 3 fragment files affected
i want to replace our EventBus usage with StateFlow/SharedFlow throughout the app. EventBus is in maybe 30 places
the ProfileActivity.java needs to become a kotlin file — it's like 800 lines of pure java with no kotlin anywhere. what's the safest approach given it has zero tests
we use Glide 4.x in the whole app but i heard coil is more idiomatic kotlin. thinking about switching image loading library in our feed module
our gradle version is ancient (6.7) and i want to upgrade to 8.x. we also need to migrate from groovy to kotlin dsl at some point
what's the difference between coroutines and rxjava? which one should i use for a new android project
can you help me debug why my StateFlow isn't emitting in the viewmodel? i set the value but the collect in the fragment never fires
i want to add a new feature to the app — a dark mode toggle. where should i put the preference storage logic in our MVVM setup
how do i write a unit test for a suspend function that calls a repository? should i use runTest or runBlocking
we have a memory leak in our fragment — the adapter is holding a reference to the activity context. how do i fix this
i want to upgrade the kotlin version from 1.8 to 2.0 in our build.gradle. are there any breaking changes i should know about
need to refactor the PaymentService class — it's gotten too big and does too many things. want to split it into smaller classes following SRP
we're migrating our backend from Spring Boot 2 to 3. the main change is jakarta namespace instead of javax. about 40 files affected
our ios team wants to share business logic with android. they heard about KMP but i'm not sure what that even means for our existing android kotlin codebase
